Off Calendar Nov-18-2024 Continued To Feb-05-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reconsider Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default And Default Judgment, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Nov-01-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Richard Hurlburt and Mujghan Ahmad; Legal Assistance to the Elderly; 415-757-4524; attorneys for Plaintiff Cynthia Byrd.

Robert G. Cummings; Law Offices of Robert G. Cummings; 650-363-7280; attorney for Defendant Cyrone Byrd.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Hearing held. Mr. Cummings argued that he informed the defendant about the potential to set aside a default within 180 days, leading to a possible misunderstanding that the response timeline was extended. Mr. Cummings cited excusable neglect due to this perceived advice and requested equitable relief to allow the defendant a hearing on the merits of the case.

Opposing counsel argued that Mr. Cummings' statements did not constitute attorney fault justifying mandatory default relief. Previous motions to set aside the default had been denied, and they asserted that there was no sufficient new evidence for reconsideration.

After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court rules as follows:

DEFENDANT CYRONE BYRD'S MOTION TO RECONSIDER MOTION TO SET ASIDE ENTRY OF DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant to file their Answer forthwith.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Shivanee Singh, CSR #13983, depos@keltyscott.com, 650-366-3008 (D501)

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Aug-20-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:

Richard Hurlburt Esq., Legal Assistance to the Edlerly, 415-757-4531, in-person for plaintiff
Robert Cummings Esq., Law office of Robert Cummings, 650-363-7280, in-person appearance for and with defendant, Cyrone Byrd

Mr. Cummings contests to the tentative ruling. Mr. Cummings's notice of contesting the tentative ruling was sent at 12:48 pm on 8/19/24. He was mis-inform by his assistance of a wrong tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CYRONE BYRD MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is DENIED. Request for mandatory relief that is referenced in the reply is not properly before the Court at this time. See Notice of Motion.

Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)
